reasoning: I estimate a 38% probability that the GISTEMP global temperature anomaly for October 2025 will be less than 1.10°C. Historically, October anomalies have only exceeded 1.10°C very recently (like the +1.34°C in October 2023). Based on the linear warming trend of ~0.25°C per decade, an expected La Niña month in late 2025 would naturally fall around 1.02°C to 1.07°C. However, since mid-2023, the global climate has experienced a persistent "step change" upwards, running about 0.15°C to 0.20°C hotter than typical models estimate (potentially driven by factors like the IMO 2020 regulations on shipping aerosols and accumulating ocean heat). If even a portion of this anomalous warmth persists into 2025, the baseline for a La Niña or neutral October would shift into the 1.15°C–1.20°C range. Because dropping below 1.10°C would require both a strong La Niña effect and significant dissipation of the recent underlying heat spike, it remains plausible but constitutes the less likely outcome.
